reason: Provenance gap: the node's only external citation is GT-kitaev-spectral, whose declared loci are approximate_algebras.tex:354-357 and 2171-2182. Those loci contain ONLY the definition tilde-Phi=theta(2Phi-1)=(1/2)(1+(2Phi-1)(1-4(Phi-Phi^2))^(-1/2)) and the bare ASSERTION of its properties (idempotence etc.) in cb-norm. They do NOT contain the function-calculus multiplicativity/homomorphism property f(X)g(X)=(fg)(X) needed to justify R^2=(S^2)^(-1). In Kitaev that homomorphism property appears only at line 552 for the HOLOMORPHIC functional calculus, which (a) is outside the cited GT loci and (b) Kitaev explicitly disclaims at line 535 ('We have not found use of these methods due to their fragility in the approximate setting'). The actually-used Taylor-series calculus (Kitaev line 503-512) states only that f(X) is well-defined, COMMUTES WITH X, and obeys a norm bound -- it does NOT assert multiplicativity. Kitaev's abs_sgn (line 515-518) does assert sgn(X)^2=I, but (a) it is stated without proof and (b) the node does not cite it. So the node relies on an unprovenanced algebraic fact.
